summary: The Pester test suite covers core happy-path functionality (parsing, mocking, compliance checks, report generation, basic error handling) but is significantly incomplete. Critical gaps: (1) No tests for requirements.txt parsing despite task requirement; (2) Missing edge cases (empty manifests, null values, duplicate dependencies, case sensitivity, invalid config); (3) Test fixtures referenced but contents unverified; (4) Integration test has no assertions on report structure/values—only counts; (5) Separate test-workflow.ps1 doesn't meet CI/CD requirements: skips actionlint validation, doesn't generate act-result.txt, doesn't parse/verify compliance report output, doesn't assert exact values. (6) Insufficient comments documenting TDD methodology or test rationale; (7) No test isolation—uses hardcoded paths and real file I/O; (8) Config file validation untested (missing allowedLicenses array, malformed JSON). The suite would miss regressions in case sensitivity, empty-input handling, manifest format variants, and workflow compliance.
